@@ -372,6 +372,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-8xu","title":"Reference: agentic_coding_flywheel implementation details","description":"# Reference Implementation Details ## Source github.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup (cloned to /tmp/jargon_reference) This bead documents the key implementation patterns to adapt for BrennerBot. --- ## JargonTerm Schema (`lib/jargon.ts`) ```typescript export interface JargonTerm { term: string; // Display name short: string; // ~1 line for tooltip long: string; // 2-4 sentences, plain English analogy?: string; // "Think of it like..." why?: string; // Why we use it / what problem it solves related?: string[]; // Related term keys } // Dictionary structure export const jargonDictionary: Record = { "term-key": { term: "Term Name", short: "...", long: "...", ... } }; // Lookup helper export function getJargon(termKey: string): JargonTerm | undefined { return jargonDictionary[termKey.toLowerCase().replace(/[\s_]+/g, "-")]; } ``` --- ## Jargon Component (`components/jargon.tsx`) ### Trigger Button Styling ```tsx {/* Content */}
``` ### Animation Configuration ```tsx // Spring animation (respect reduced motion) const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion(); // Tooltip entrance initial: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: position === "top" ? 8 : -8, scale: 0.95 } animate: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 1 } : { opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 } transition: prefersReducedMotion ? { duration: 0.12 } : springs.snappy // Sheet entrance initial: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 0 } : { y: "100%" } animate: prefersReducedMotion ? { opacity: 1 } : { y: 0 } transition: prefersReducedMotion ? { duration: 0.12 } : springs.smooth ``` --- ## Glossary Page (`app/glossary/page.tsx`) ### Category System ```tsx type GlossaryCategory = "all" | "shell" | "networking" | "tools" | "concepts"; function categorizeKey(key: string): Exclude { // Regex patterns to auto-categorize based on term key } ``` For BrennerBot, adapt to: ```tsx type GlossaryCategory = "all" | "operators" | "brenner" | "biology" | "bayesian" | "method" | "project"; ``` ### Search Implementation ```tsx function buildSearchable(entry): string { return [ entry.key, entry.term, entry.short, entry.long, entry.analogy ?? "", entry.why ?? "", ...(entry.related ?? []), ].join(" ").toLowerCase(); } // Filter const filtered = entries.filter((entry) => { if (category !== "all" && entry.category !== category) return false; if (query.length === 0) return true; return entry.searchable.includes(query); }); ``` ### Deep Linking ```tsx useEffect(() => { const openFromHash = () => { const raw = window.location.hash.replace(/^#/, ""); if (!raw) return; const key = decodeURIComponent(raw); const target = document.getElementById(key); if (!target) return; // Open
if applicable if (target instanceof HTMLDetailsElement) { target.open = true; } else { const details = target.closest("details"); if (details) details.open = true; } target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" }); }; openFromHash(); window.addEventListener("hashchange", openFromHash); return () => window.removeEventListener("hashchange", openFromHash); }, []); ``` ### Term Card (expandable details) ```tsx

{entry.term}

#{entry.key} {category}

{entry.short}
{/* Full definition */}

What it means

{entry.long}

{/* Why box (green accent) */} {entry.why && (

Why we use it

{entry.why}

)} {/* Analogy box (primary accent) */} {entry.analogy && (

Think of it like...

{entry.analogy}

)} {/* Related terms */} {entry.related?.length > 0 && (

Related terms

{entry.related.map((key) => ( {key} ))}
)}
``` --- ## Adaptation Notes for BrennerBot ### Styling Changes 1. Replace `oklch` gradient colors with brenner design tokens 2. Use existing CSS animations from globals.css instead of framer-motion springs 3. Match rounded-xl pattern from existing Card component 4. Use existing primary/accent/muted-foreground tokens ### Structural Changes 1. Categories: operators, brenner, biology, bayesian, method, project 2. Add links to transcript §n anchors for biology terms 3. Connect to /corpus/[doc] routes for "learn more" 4. No motion library - use CSS animations or simple transitions ### Missing from Reference (add for BrennerBot) 1. Category icons (different icon per category) 2. Transcript anchor links in "why" sections 3. Operator symbol in term display for operator category --- ## Files to Create 1. `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` - Types + dictionary + helper 2. `apps/web/src/components/jargon.tsx` - Tooltip/sheet component 3. `apps/web/src/app/glossary/page.tsx` - Full glossary page 4. Consider: `apps/web/src/hooks/useReducedMotion.ts` - Motion preference hook","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:00:21.322247456-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:25:12.905543812-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:25:12.905543812-05:00","close_reason":"Reference implementation fully documented in earlier session - analyzed jargon.tsx, jargon.ts, and glossary/page.tsx from agentic_coding_flywheel_setup repository","labels":["documentation","glossary","reference"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8yn","title":"Create base layout with navigation and footer","description":"# Task Build the root layout component with navigation header and footer. # Navigation Header - Logo/brand mark (left) - Main nav links: Corpus, Distillations, Method, Sessions - Theme toggle (optional, dark is default) - Mobile hamburger menu # Footer - Brief description - Links: GitHub repo, Agent Mail, Anthropic/OpenAI/Google - Copyright/attribution note # Layout Structure ```tsx
{children}
``` # Technical Notes - Use Next.js App Router layout.tsx - Navigation should use next/link for client-side routing - Mobile menu should be accessible (keyboard, screen reader) - Sticky header with backdrop blur effect # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Navigation renders with all links - [ ] Active link state indicated - [ ] Mobile menu works correctly - [ ] Footer renders with proper links - [ ] Layout responsive at all breakpoints","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:36.213575567-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:38:34.972578809-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:38:34.972578809-05:00","close_reason":"Base layout implemented - navigation with Corpus/Distillations/Method links, theme-aware setup, fluid spacing tokens, footer with external links","labels":["design-system","layout","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.35742951-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-84o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.387599579-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-dfg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.417709063-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:59.482427128-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-90k","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize TranscriptViewer Sections","description":"# Goal Virtualize the TranscriptViewer to only render visible sections, reducing DOM nodes from 236 to ~10. ## Why Priority 0? The transcript is the MOST viewed page. It contains 236 sections. Currently all render at once causing: - Slow initial paint (3+ seconds on slow devices) - Janky scrolling - High memory usage - Poor Core Web Vitals This is the single highest-impact performance optimization. ## Current Implementation ```typescript // TranscriptViewer.tsx {data.sections.map((section, index) => (
{ sectionRefs.current[index] = el; }}>
))} ``` This renders ALL 236 sections into the DOM. ## Target Implementation ```typescript import { useVirtualizer } from '@tanstack/react-virtual'; function TranscriptViewer({ data }) { const parentRef = useRef(null); const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({ count: data.sections.length, getScrollElement: () => parentRef.current, estimateSize: () => 200, // Estimated section height overscan: 3, // Render 3 extra above/below viewport }); return (
{virtualizer.getVirtualItems().map((virtualRow) => { const section = data.sections[virtualRow.index]; return (
); })}
); } ``` ## Key Challenges ### 1. Variable Height Sections Sections have different heights (some are 2 lines, some are 20). Solution: Use `measureElement` for dynamic measurement. ### 2. Scroll Position Restoration When navigating back, should restore scroll position. Solution: Integrate with TanStack Store (later task). ### 3. Active Section Tracking Currently uses IntersectionObserver to track which section is visible. Solution: Virtual provides `scrollToIndex` and we can derive active from virtualizer state. ### 4. TOC Navigation Clicking TOC item should scroll to that section. Solution: `virtualizer.scrollToIndex(index, { align: 'start' })` ### 5. URL Hash Links Direct links like `/corpus/transcript#section-42` should work. Solution: On mount, parse hash and scroll to index. ## Considerations ### Scroll Container Transcript uses `window` scroll currently. Virtual works best with a dedicated scroll container. Trade-off: Slightly different feel, but much better performance. ### SSR Compatibility Virtual is client-only. Need to handle hydration: - Option A: Render first N sections on server, virtualize on client - Option B: Render placeholder, full virtualize on client ### Reading Progress The ReadingProgress component calculates scroll percentage. With virtualization, calculate from `virtualizer.scrollOffset` instead. ## Performance Metrics Before: - 236 DOM nodes for sections - ~3s initial render on slow devices After: - ~13 DOM nodes for sections (10 visible + 3 overscan) - <500ms initial render - 95% reduction in DOM size ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Virtual - [ ] Only visible sections render (verify in DevTools) - [ ] Scrolling is smooth at 60fps - [ ] TOC navigation works - [ ] URL hash links work - [ ] Reading progress still works - [ ] Active section highlighting works ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx - apps/web/src/components/transcript/ReadingProgress.tsx (adapt to virtual) - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TableOfContents.tsx (scrollToIndex)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:18.733131224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:51:38.631954928-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:51:38.631954928-05:00","close_reason":"Virtualization implemented with useVirtualizer, build and tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-90k","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:47.425211258-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-93bz","title":"Fix pending ACK detection to be case-insensitive in thread status","description":"Pending ACK detection should treat recipient names case-insensitively so replies from differently-cased agents clear the pending list. Update threadStatus ack logic to normalize names before comparison and keep display names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T10:24:21.407150806-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T10:26:11.525715502-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T10:26:11.525715502-05:00","close_reason":"Normalize pending ACK detection case-insensitively and add regression test","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-93bz","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-05T10:24:21.433024992-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-95h","title":"Task: Screenshot all pages at mobile widths","description":"Capture screenshots of every page at 375px, 414px, and 768px widths. ## Purpose Create a visual baseline and identify issues. This is detective work before fixing. ## Pages to Screenshot 1. Home page (/) 2. Corpus index (/corpus) 3. Corpus document pages (all 7 documents) 4. Distillations index (/distillations) 5. Distillations compare (/distillations/compare) 6. Method page (/method) 7. Sessions/new (/sessions/new) - if lab mode enabled ## Widths - 375px - iPhone SE/Mini (smallest common iPhone) - 414px - iPhone Plus/Pro Max (larger iPhone) - 768px - iPad portrait (tablet) ## Output - Screenshots saved in organized folder structure - Light AND dark mode for each - Both portrait orientation - Full-page captures (not just viewport) ## Tool Options - Playwright screenshot API (recommended - scriptable) - Chrome DevTools device mode - BrowserStack for real devices (if available) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All pages captured at all 3 widths - [ ] Light and dark mode variants - [ ] Full-page screenshots (not truncated) - [ ] Organized naming: `{page}_{width}_{theme}.png`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:58.242896399-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:04:14.974362059-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:04:14.974362059-05:00","close_reason":"Screenshots captured as part of 3vw audit - 30+ screenshots at 375/414/768px in light/dark modes","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-95h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-3vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:28:36.49626611-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-976","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize CompareView Dual Panes","description":"# Goal Virtualize both panes of the distillation comparison view for smooth synchronized scrolling. ## Background CompareView displays two distillations side-by-side: - Each distillation is 13-38KB of parsed content - Users scroll both panes, optionally synchronized - Current implementation renders all content in both panes ## Current Implementation ```typescript // CompareView.tsx
{data.parts.map(part => ( ))} {data.parts.map(part => ( ))}
``` ## Challenges Specific to Compare View ### 1. Synchronized Scrolling When syncScroll is enabled, both panes scroll together. Virtual complicates this because: - Items may have different heights in each pane - Need to sync by content position, not pixel offset Solution approach: ```typescript // Sync by percentage, not pixels const leftPercent = leftVirtualizer.scrollOffset / leftVirtualizer.getTotalSize(); rightVirtualizer.scrollToOffset(leftPercent * rightVirtualizer.getTotalSize()); ``` ### 2. Different Content Lengths Opus distillation: ~38KB GPT distillation: ~26KB Gemini distillation: ~13KB Panes have different total heights. Sync should handle this gracefully. ### 3. Scroll Position per Model Remember scroll position for each model selection. When user switches from Opus to GPT, restore GPT's last position. ## Implementation ### 1. Create virtualized pane component ```typescript function VirtualizedPane({ model, data, scrollRef }) { const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({ count: data.sections.length, getScrollElement: () => scrollRef.current, estimateSize: () => 300, }); // Expose virtualizer for sync useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({ scrollToOffset: virtualizer.scrollToOffset, scrollOffset: virtualizer.scrollOffset, totalSize: virtualizer.getTotalSize(), })); return (/* virtual list */); } ``` ### 2. Implement sync controller ```typescript function useSyncScroll(leftRef, rightRef, enabled) { useEffect(() => { if (!enabled) return; const handleLeftScroll = () => { const percent = leftRef.current.scrollOffset / leftRef.current.totalSize; rightRef.current.scrollToOffset(percent * rightRef.current.totalSize); }; // Attach scroll listeners, debounce, etc. }, [enabled]); } ``` ## Considerations ### Mobile View On mobile, only one pane shows at a time (tabs). Virtualization still helps but sync doesn't apply. ### Bookmark/Highlight Sync Future feature: highlight same section in both panes. Virtual doesn't block this but design should account for it. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Both panes virtualized - [ ] Sync scroll works smoothly - [ ] Independent scroll works when sync disabled - [ ] Model switching preserves position - [ ] Mobile (single pane) still works - [ ] Smooth 60fps scrolling ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/VirtualizedPane.tsx (create) - apps/web/src/hooks/useSyncScroll.ts (create)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:17:47.481616393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:54:44.21691739-05:00","close_reason":"Feature removed - compare view doesn't make sense","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-976","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-90k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:13.6361465-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-99em","title":"Bug: Section reference regex is stateful across calls","description":"`apps/web/src/components/section-reference.tsx` exports a global `/.../g` regex and uses `.test()` without resetting `lastIndex`, which can yield false negatives on repeated calls. Fix by resetting `lastIndex` before `.test()` and `matchAll()` usage. Acceptance: - `hasSectionReferences()` is stable across repeated calls - `cd apps/web && bun run test` passes ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenMountain","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:47:39.230366192-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T12:22:04.579588367-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T12:22:04.579588367-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (reset global regex lastIndex; tests passing)","labels":["ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-99em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:47:39.254728671-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -667,7 +668,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","title":"Define Agent Roles & System Prompts","description":"# Define Agent Roles & System Prompts ## Purpose Create the distinct agent personas and their system prompts that drive the Tribunal experience. ## The Three Tribunal Agents ### 🔴 Devil's Advocate **Mandate**: Find holes, steelman alternatives, be maximally skeptical **System Prompt Elements**: - Never agree too quickly - Challenge every assumption - Propose the strongest alternative explanations - Point out hidden assumptions - Steelman opposing views - Be intellectually aggressive but fair **Tone**: Skeptical, probing, intellectually rigorous ### 🟢 Experiment Designer **Mandate**: Propose concrete, feasible study protocols **System Prompt Elements**: - Focus on operationalization - Generate specific experimental designs - Calculate sample sizes and power - Consider practical constraints - Address ethical implications - Propose both ideal and realistic versions **Tone**: Practical, methodical, constructive ### 🟡 Brenner Channeler **Mandate**: Respond as Sydney Brenner would **System Prompt Elements**: - Channel Brenner's voice and thinking style - Reference specific transcript sections - Be provocative and direct - Challenge sloppy thinking - Push toward discriminative testing - Use Brenner's actual language patterns **Tone**: Blunt, witty, intellectually demanding ## Prompt Files ``` apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/agents/prompts/ ├── devils-advocate.md ├── experiment-designer.md ├── brenner-channeler.md └── synthesis.md ``` ## Prompt Template Structure Each prompt should include: 1. Role description 2. Mandate and goals 3. Tone and style guidance 4. Example responses 5. Constraints (what NOT to do) 6. Input format (hypothesis, context) 7. Output format (structured response) ## Testing Prompts should be tested with: - Sample hypotheses from different domains - Edge cases (vague hypotheses, controversial topics) - Consistency checks (multiple runs) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All three agent prompts defined - [ ] Prompts produce distinct, useful responses - [ ] Brenner Channeler sounds like Brenner - [ ] Devil's Advocate is genuinely challenging - [ ] Experiment Designer is practical ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.243574221-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:44:49.175500355-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:44:49.175500355-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all four tribunal agent prompts with TypeScript exports","labels":["brenner-loop","multi-agent","prompts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.277039731-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","title":"Implement Agent Dispatch via Agent Mail","description":"# Implement Agent Dispatch via Agent Mail ## Purpose Create the system for dispatching hypothesis analysis requests to agents via Agent Mail. ## Why Agent Mail - No vendor AI APIs in web app (per AGENTS.md) - Agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) access via their MCP tools - Auditability: all conversations persisted in git - Async by design: agents can take time ## Flow 1. **Web App Creates Thread** - New thread for the session - Include hypothesis, operator results, context 2. **Dispatch Messages to Agents** - One message per agent role - Include role-specific prompt - Mark with appropriate importance 3. **Agents Respond** - Each agent processes via their coding agent - Responses come back as mail messages - May be async (polling required) 4. **Collect & Display Responses** - Web app polls for new messages - Display as responses arrive - Show status (pending/complete) ## Implementation ```typescript interface AgentDispatch { sessionId: string; threadId: string; hypothesis: HypothesisCard; operatorResults: OperatorResults; tasks: AgentTask[]; responses: AgentResponse[]; } interface AgentTask { role: AgentRole; messageId?: string; status: 'pending' | 'dispatched' | 'received' | 'error'; dispatchedAt?: Date; response?: AgentResponse; } interface AgentResponse { role: AgentRole; content: string; receivedAt: Date; messageId: string; } ``` ## Agent Mail Integration Using MCP tools from web app's perspective: - Create thread: via API or existing integration - Send messages: `send_message` with role-specific content - Poll inbox: `fetch_inbox` for responses - Mark read: `mark_message_read` ## Fallback When No Agents If agents aren't registered or responding: - Show "Agent Tribunal Unavailable" status - Provide curated Brenner quotes instead - Suggest self-reflection questions - Don't block session progress ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/agents/dispatch.ts` `apps/web/src/hooks/useAgentDispatch.ts` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Can dispatch to registered agents - [ ] Responses collected and displayed - [ ] Polling works for async responses - [ ] Graceful fallback when unavailable ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.553067774-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T14:01:22.987584455-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T14:01:22.987584455-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Agent Dispatch via Agent Mail - created dispatch.ts with core dispatch logic and useAgentDispatch React hook","labels":["agent-mail","brenner-loop","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.554356249-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:12.991067483-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.3","title":"Build Agent Tribunal UI Panel","description":"# Build Agent Tribunal UI Panel ## Purpose Create the UI that displays agent responses during the tribunal phase. ## Design ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ AGENT TRIBUNAL │ │ Your hypothesis is being evaluated by multiple perspectives│ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🔴 DEVIL'S ADVOCATE [Complete] │ │ │ │ Role: Find holes, steelman alternatives │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ "Your hypothesis assumes teenagers are passive │ │ │ │ receivers of content. But they CHOOSE what to │ │ │ │ engage with. The algorithm shows you what you've │ │ │ │ already indicated you want..." │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Expand Full Response] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🟢 EXPERIMENT DESIGNER [Complete] │ │ │ │ Role: Propose concrete protocols │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ "I propose a three-arm randomized trial: │ │ │ │ Arm A: Normal algorithmic feed..." │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Expand Full Response] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🟡 BRENNER CHANNELER [Analyzing] │ │ │ │ Role: What would Sydney Brenner say? │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ [Loading animation...] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ [View Full Responses] [Generate Synthesis] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## Components ### AgentTribunalPanel Main container showing all three agents. ### AgentCard Individual agent response card: ```typescript interface AgentCardProps { role: AgentRole; status: 'pending' | 'analyzing' | 'complete' | 'error'; response?: string; preview?: string; // Truncated preview onExpand: () => void; } ``` ### AgentResponseModal Full response view in modal. ### AgentStatusIndicator Visual indicator of agent status. ## Status States - **Pending**: Waiting to dispatch (gray, empty) - **Analyzing**: Request sent, waiting (animated) - **Complete**: Response received (green, content shown) - **Error**: Failed to respond (red, retry option) ## Animations - Smooth expansion of response text - Loading animation for analyzing state - Staggered card appearance ## File Location `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/agents/AgentTribunalPanel.tsx` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/agents/AgentCard.tsx` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/agents/AgentResponseModal.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Three agent cards displayed - [ ] Status indicators work correctly - [ ] Responses display properly - [ ] Expand/collapse works - [ ] Handles error states gracefully ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.837556003-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T10:17:43.392819385-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T10:17:43.392819385-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Agent Tribunal UI panel + tribunal thread view","labels":["brenner-loop","multi-agent","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:26.838828157-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:13.037879859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:33.357820458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","title":"Implement Response Synthesis & Conflict Detection","description":"# Implement Response Synthesis & Conflict Detection ## Purpose Analyze agent responses to identify points of agreement, disagreement, and generate a unified synthesis. ## Why Synthesis Matters Three separate agent responses are valuable, but users also need: - What do agents AGREE on? - Where do they DISAGREE? - What are the key takeaways? ## Synthesis Structure ```typescript interface SynthesisResult { // Agreement consensusPoints: ConsensusPoint[]; // Conflict conflictPoints: ConflictPoint[]; // Recommendations recommendations: Recommendation[]; // Brenner Principles Applied brennerPrinciples: AppliedPrinciple[]; // Metadata synthesizedAt: Date; agentResponses: AgentResponse[]; } interface ConsensusPoint { claim: string; supportingAgents: AgentRole[]; strength: 'strong' | 'moderate' | 'weak'; } interface ConflictPoint { topic: string; positions: { agent: AgentRole; position: string }[]; resolution?: string; } interface Recommendation { action: string; priority: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; rationale: string; suggestedBy: AgentRole[]; } ``` ## Synthesis Logic Can be done with: 1. **Heuristic Approach**: Pattern matching, keyword extraction 2. **Agent-Based**: Have a "Synthesis Agent" analyze other responses 3. **Hybrid**: Heuristics + optional LLM refinement Start with heuristics, add agent-based later. ### Heuristic Approach - Extract key claims from each response - Compare claims for overlap (agreement) - Identify contradictions (conflict) - Surface actionable recommendations ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/agents/synthesis.ts` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Consensus points extracted - [ ] Conflicts identified - [ ] Recommendations surfaced - [ ] Synthesis is useful and readable ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.115604188-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.115604188-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","multi-agent","synthesis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.116798215-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:13.077471061-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","title":"Implement Response Synthesis & Conflict Detection","description":"# Implement Response Synthesis & Conflict Detection ## Purpose Analyze agent responses to identify points of agreement, disagreement, and generate a unified synthesis. ## Why Synthesis Matters Three separate agent responses are valuable, but users also need: - What do agents AGREE on? - Where do they DISAGREE? - What are the key takeaways? ## Synthesis Structure ```typescript interface SynthesisResult { // Agreement consensusPoints: ConsensusPoint[]; // Conflict conflictPoints: ConflictPoint[]; // Recommendations recommendations: Recommendation[]; // Brenner Principles Applied brennerPrinciples: AppliedPrinciple[]; // Metadata synthesizedAt: Date; agentResponses: AgentResponse[]; } interface ConsensusPoint { claim: string; supportingAgents: AgentRole[]; strength: 'strong' | 'moderate' | 'weak'; } interface ConflictPoint { topic: string; positions: { agent: AgentRole; position: string }[]; resolution?: string; } interface Recommendation { action: string; priority: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; rationale: string; suggestedBy: AgentRole[]; } ``` ## Synthesis Logic Can be done with: 1. **Heuristic Approach**: Pattern matching, keyword extraction 2. **Agent-Based**: Have a "Synthesis Agent" analyze other responses 3. **Hybrid**: Heuristics + optional LLM refinement Start with heuristics, add agent-based later. ### Heuristic Approach - [ ] Consensus points extracted - [ ] Conflicts identified - [ ] Recommendations surfaced - [ ] Synthesis is useful and readable ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.115604188-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T10:25:58.006462038-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","multi-agent","synthesis"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.116798215-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:13.077471061-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.5","title":"Create Brenner Channeler Agent Persona","description":"# Create Brenner Channeler Agent Persona ## Purpose Build the special "Brenner Would Say" agent that responds in character based on the transcript. ## Why This Agent is Special The Brenner Channeler isn't just another critique agent. It channels Sydney Brenner's actual voice, drawing on: - His specific turns of phrase - His intellectual style - His favorite examples - His emphasis on discrimination vs confirmation ## Character Elements ### Voice & Style From transcript analysis: - Blunt, direct communication - Wry humor, occasional sarcasm - Reference to personal experiences - Analogy-heavy explanations - Provocative questions - Impatience with sloppy thinking ### Core Themes to Emphasize - "Not merely unlikely — impossible" - The chastity control concept - Level separation - Mechanism vs correlation - The value of being proven wrong ### Example Response Style ``` User: "I found a correlation of r=0.3, p<0.05. Is my hypothesis confirmed?" Brenner Channeler: "Confirmed? You've done nothing of the sort. You've found that your data is not wildly inconsistent with your hypothesis. That's a much weaker statement. r=0.3 means you're explaining 9% of the variance. What's explaining the other 91%? That's where the interesting science is. And p<0.05 just means you probably didn't get this result by chance. It says nothing about whether your mechanism is correct. A dozen different mechanisms could produce r=0.3. What you need is an observation that would be IMPOSSIBLE if you're wrong. Not merely unlikely — impossible. When you find that, come back and we'll talk about 'confirmation.' Until then, you have a correlation in search of a mechanism, which is the very first step, not the last." — Based on §89, §42, §73 ``` ## Implementation - System prompt trained on transcript examples - Citations back to specific sections - Consistent persona across responses ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/agents/prompts/brenner-channeler.md` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Responses sound like Brenner - [ ] Citations to transcript included - [ ] Core themes consistently emphasized - [ ] Tone is appropriately provocative ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.407238689-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T04:43:27.611828055-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T04:43:27.611828055-05:00","close_reason":"Strengthened Brenner Channeler prompt + citable quote bank","labels":["brenner-channeler","brenner-loop","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:30:27.408385428-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:13.115062097-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.6","title":"FEATURE: Objection Register - Track & Address Agent Challenges","description":"# Objection Register - Track & Address Agent Challenges ## The Gap This Fills The Devil's Advocate and other agents raise objections, but currently: - Objections are buried in prose responses - No tracking of which objections have been addressed - No accountability for unaddressed challenges - Easy to ignore uncomfortable critiques ## Feature Design ### Objection Data Model ```typescript interface Objection { id: string; sessionId: string; hypothesisId: string; // Source source: AgentRole | 'user' | 'external'; sourceMessageId?: string; // Link to original agent response // Content type: ObjectionType; summary: string; // One-line summary fullArgument: string; // Complete objection implications: string[]; // What follows if objection is valid // Severity severity: 'fatal' | 'serious' | 'moderate' | 'minor'; // Status status: ObjectionStatus; response?: ObjectionResponse; // Metadata raisedAt: Date; addressedAt?: Date; } type ObjectionType = | 'alternative_explanation' // Y might be caused by Z instead | 'reverse_causation' // Y might cause X | 'confound_identified' // Unmeasured variable problem | 'measurement_issue' // How you're measuring is flawed | 'selection_bias' // Who you're studying is biased | 'logic_error' // Reasoning doesn't follow | 'missing_evidence' // Claim not supported | 'effect_size_concern' // Too small to matter | 'generalization_problem' // Doesn't apply broadly | 'other'; type ObjectionStatus = | 'open' // Not yet addressed | 'acknowledged' // Recognized but not resolved | 'addressed' // Response provided | 'dismissed' // Explained why not relevant | 'accepted' // Objection valid, hypothesis updated | 'testing' // Being investigated interface ObjectionResponse { responseType: 'refuted' | 'accepted' | 'partially_accepted' | 'testing' | 'dismissed'; argument: string; evidence?: string; hypothesisUpdated: boolean; confidenceImpact?: number; // How much did this change confidence? } ``` ### Key Features #### 1. Automatic Extraction Parse agent responses to extract objections: - Identify challenge statements - Classify by type - Rate severity - Create register entries #### 2. Objection Dashboard ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ OBJECTION REGISTER 3 open, 2 addressed │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ ⚠️ OPEN (requires response) │ │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🔴 FATAL: Reverse Causation │ │ │ │ "Depression may cause increased social media use, │ │ │ │ not the reverse" — Devil's Advocate │ │ │ │ [Address] [Accept] [Dismiss with reason] │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🟡 SERIOUS: Selection Bias │ │ │ │ "Users who report depression may have different │ │ │ │ usage patterns pre-existing" — Brenner Channeler │ │ │ │ [Address] [Accept] [Dismiss with reason] │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ ✅ ADDRESSED │ │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ ✓ Measurement Issue: "Self-report is unreliable" │ │ │ │ Response: Refuted — Using validated PHQ-9 instrument │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` #### 3. Objection-Driven Testing Each objection can spawn a test: - "To address the reverse causation objection, I need a test that..." - Links objection to test queue - Resolves objection when test completes #### 4. Research Brief Integration Research Brief must include: - All objections raised - How each was addressed (or not) - Outstanding objections clearly marked - Intellectual honesty score #### 5. Objection Patterns Track across sessions: - "This hypothesis type frequently faces reverse causation objections" - "Users who dismiss objections have lower robustness scores" ### Why This is Critical Without objection tracking: - Users can ignore valid challenges - Confirmation bias goes unchecked - Research Brief lacks accountability - The tribunal's value is diminished With it: - Every challenge is on the record - Responses are documented - Unaddressed objections are visible - Intellectual honesty is enforced ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Objections extracted from agent responses - [ ] Each objection requires explicit handling - [ ] Responses are recorded and visible - [ ] Research Brief shows objection status - [ ] Outstanding objections prevent "complete" status ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:40:39.596021286-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:40:39.596021286-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","multi-agent","objections"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:40:39.631375171-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:47:37.245793055-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.7","title":"FEATURE: Agent Debate Mode - Multi-Round Adversarial Dialogue","description":"# Agent Debate Mode - Multi-Round Adversarial Dialogue ## The Gap This Fills Current design: Dispatch all agents → Wait → Collect responses → Synthesize But this misses the value of DEBATE: - Devil's Advocate challenges Experiment Designer's protocol - Brenner Channeler responds to Devil's Advocate's objection - Back-and-forth sharpens the analysis ## Feature Design ### Debate Structure ```typescript interface AgentDebate { id: string; sessionId: string; topic: string; // What's being debated // Participants participants: AgentRole[]; moderator: 'system' | AgentRole; // Rounds rounds: DebateRound[]; maxRounds: number; currentRound: number; // Outcome status: 'in_progress' | 'concluded' | 'timed_out'; conclusion?: DebateConclusion; } interface DebateRound { number: number; speaker: AgentRole; content: string; replyingTo?: string; // Which previous statement // Analysis newPointsMade: string[]; objectionsRaised: string[]; concessionsGiven: string[]; } interface DebateConclusion { consensus: string[]; // What all agents agreed on unresolved: string[]; // Genuine disagreements keyInsight: string; // Most important takeaway winningArguments: string[];// Strongest points made } ``` ### Key Features #### 1. Structured Debate Formats **Format A: Oxford-Style** - Motion: "This hypothesis is falsifiable" - Proposition: Experiment Designer argues for - Opposition: Devil's Advocate argues against - Judge: Brenner Channeler **Format B: Socratic Questioning** - Brenner Channeler asks probing questions - Other agents must respond - Reveals weak points through dialogue **Format C: Steel-Manning Contest** - Each agent must steelman the hypothesis - Then each agent tries to defeat their own steelman - Best attack wins #### 2. Debate Viewer ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ AGENT DEBATE: Is this hypothesis falsifiable? │ │ Round 3 of 5 │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ 🟢 Experiment Designer (Round 1): │ │ "I propose a three-arm RCT that would definitively test..." │ │ │ │ 🔴 Devil's Advocate (Round 2): │ │ "Your RCT has a fatal flaw: you can't blind participants │ │ to which arm they're in. They'll know if they're getting │ │ the algorithmic feed or not. Demand characteristics │ │ invalidate the design." │ │ │ │ 🟢 Experiment Designer (Round 3): │ │ "Fair point. I modify my proposal: instead of removing │ │ the algorithm, we modify it subtly. Participants won't │ │ know which variant they're receiving..." │ │ │ │ ⏳ Waiting for 🔴 Devil's Advocate... │ │ │ │ [Pause Debate] [Skip to Conclusion] [Inject Question] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` #### 3. User Injection User can inject questions or challenges mid-debate: - "Devil's Advocate, what about selection bias?" - Forces the debate to address specific concerns #### 4. Debate Outcomes After debate concludes: - Extract all objections to Objection Register - Extract all experimental designs - Identify where debate resolved disagreement - Flag persistent disagreements ### Integration Points - Agent Tribunal → Enhanced with debate option - Objection Register → Populated from debate - Test Queue → Experimental designs added ## Why This is Critical Single-round responses are shallow. Debate: - Sharpens arguments through opposition - Reveals weaknesses in real-time - Produces refined experimental designs - More closely mirrors how good research happens ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Multiple debate formats available - [ ] Multi-round exchanges work - [ ] User can inject questions - [ ] Conclusions properly extracted - [ ] Debate transcript preserved ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:42.727446681-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:42.727446681-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","debate","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:41:42.757874803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:47:41.21738488-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
